title: Ledger CTO: EU's MiCA Rules Are Crushing Crypto Startups
summary: Ledger's CTO warns that the EU's MiCA regulations are imposing steep compliance costs, stifling innovation for small crypto startups. The financial barriers are making it difficult for early-stage companies to survive in Europe's crypto market.
body:
Ledger's Chief Technology Officer has sounded the alarm over the European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulations, stating that the stringent compliance requirements are crippling small crypto startups. The CTO argues that the financial and operational burdens of adhering to MiCA are disproportionately affecting early-stage companies, making it nearly impossible for them to compete.

The MiCA framework, which came into full effect earlier this year, imposes rigorous licensing and reporting requirements on crypto firms. These regulations, while aimed at enhancing consumer protection and reducing financial crime, require significant investment in legal and compliance teams. For small startups, these costs can be prohibitive, leading to a wave of shutdowns and relocations to more crypto-friendly jurisdictions.

For everyday users, this could mean fewer innovative crypto products and services available in Europe. The exodus of startups could also lead to a brain drain, as talented developers and entrepreneurs seek more favorable regulatory environments. The long-term impact on Europe's position as a hub for crypto innovation remains to be seen, but the immediate effect is a chilling atmosphere for new entrants.

Startups and investors should closely monitor the EU's regulatory stance and potential adjustments to MiCA. If the costs remain high, more companies may choose to operate outside the EU, potentially leading to a fragmented crypto market. For users, staying informed about where their favorite crypto services are based could become increasingly important.
twitterThread: The EU's MiCA rules are crushing crypto startups, says Ledger's CTO. Compliance costs are too high for small firms to bear.
MiCA's strict licensing and reporting requirements demand heavy investment in legal and compliance teams, a burden for early-stage companies.

If MiCA's costs don't ease, expect more startups to leave Europe. Stay informed about where your crypto services are based. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-08-ledger-cto-eus-mica-rules-are-crushing-crypto-startups
